title: vachellia tortilis subsp. tortilis
intro: Vachellia tortilis subsp. tortilis, commonly known as the umbrella thorn or Acacia tortilis, is a tree species native to the savannas and grasslands of sub-Saharan Africa. It is a deciduous tree characterized by its broad, flat crown and thorny branches, which provide shelter and food for various animals. The medicinal properties of Vachellia tortilis subsp. tortilis include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
